## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Cycle-driven debug. The five-step depot-identity-collapse implementation landed
  (commits ee816373 → d2dec554). Remaining work is verifying it against live GCP
  by stepping through what RELEASE.md *would* prescribe — except this pace does
  not have the full RELEASE.md tabtarget set yet (canonical entry point `rbw-tP`
  is unbuilt; step-3 inner sequence beyond the pristine-lifecycle fixture is
  unreachable). Cycles run against the buildable subset.
  
  Load-bearing discipline: each turn does exactly one cycle — execute the next
  reachable step, surface what fails, diagnose root cause, recommend fix shape,
  then stop. No auto-fix. The stop keeps the operator in the loop and prevents
  the drift where mechanical fixes paper over architectural questions.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Step through reachable RELEASE.md-equivalent today: Payor health probe →
    marshal-zero + commit → pristine-lifecycle fixture (cases 1, 2, 3).
  - Out-of-reach this pace: canonical infrastructure setup, supply chain
    (inscribe/ordain), Crucible suite, cleanup. Those become reachable once
    `rbw-tP` is drafted in a follow-on pace.
  - Per surfaced issue, route disposition: notch into BBAAM if in-scope-blocking
    the verification path, slate as separate pace if structural or out-of-scope,
    capture as itch if speculative.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract (waived to main for
    initial debug per operator).
  - No fix lands without explicit operator nod after diagnosis is described.
  - Diagnosed-but-unfixed issues accumulate as scar candidates for wrap-time
    routing.

Filesystem is the data bus — no third intermediate (the initial draft proposed a ZRBGP_DEPOT_STATE_SUMMARY pipe-separated file, dropped after review questioned its necessity). 4a66a4ce  ₢BBAAM    Step 2 follow-on cleanup of rbgp_depot_levy surfaced by post-edit review. (1) Auth/region consolidation: the 'Validate input parameters' wrapping step (renamed to 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' in the prior notch) authenticated once at line 578 to call the AR locations API for region validation, then the next step 'Authenticate as Payor' authenticated again at line 597 with a duplicate 'local z_token' declaration and identical zrbgp_authenticate_capture call — the first token was unused after the region check. One auth, one token, dependency between the two steps now explicit (region-check requires authenticated AR access). (2) Dead RBRP env-var checks dropped: the three 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die ...' / RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID / RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID lines under the old 'Authenticate as Payor' step were belt-and-suspenders dead code — zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish already validates these are non-empty before any rbgp command runs (rbgp_cli.sh line 63), and Step 2 unconditionalizing zrbrr_enforce makes the analogous-pattern asymmetry stand out. Removed all three. (3) Joint-length validation added at the right layer: zrbrr_enforce (rbrr_regime.sh) gains a custom check after the CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX format checks asserting $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} )) -le 30, with a buc_die message naming each component's length and the joint sum so an operator can immediately see which knob to dial back. The previous inline 30-char guard in levy fired only after auth + region API + project body construction; the regime-kindle layer catches the failure before any GCP API call. Worst-case math under existing individual caps: CLOUD_PREFIX max 11 (incl. trailing hyphen) + 'd-'(2) + MONIKER max 26 = 39 chars; the joint cap excludes the 30..39 sliver from regime validity. Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Cycle-driven debug. The five-step depot-identity-collapse implementation landed
  (commits ee816373 → d2dec554). Remaining work is verifying it against live GCP
  by stepping through what RELEASE.md *would* prescribe — except this pace does
  not have the full RELEASE.md tabtarget set yet (canonical entry point `rbw-tP`
  is unbuilt; step-3 inner sequence beyond the pristine-lifecycle fixture is
  unreachable). Cycles run against the buildable subset.
  
  Load-bearing discipline: each turn does exactly one cycle — execute the next
  reachable step, surface what fails, diagnose root cause, recommend fix shape,
  then stop. No auto-fix. The stop keeps the operator in the loop and prevents
  the drift where mechanical fixes paper over architectural questions.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Step through reachable RELEASE.md-equivalent today: Payor health probe →
    marshal-zero + commit → pristine-lifecycle fixture (cases 1, 2, 3).
  - Out-of-reach this pace: canonical infrastructure setup, supply chain
    (inscribe/ordain), Crucible suite, cleanup. Those become reachable once
    `rbw-tP` is drafted in a follow-on pace.
  - Per surfaced issue, route disposition: notch into BBAAM if in-scope-blocking
    the verification path, slate as separate pace if structural or out-of-scope,
    capture as itch if speculative.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract (waived to main for
    initial debug per operator).
  - No fix lands without explicit operator nod after diagnosis is described.
  - Diagnosed-but-unfixed issues accumulate as scar candidates for wrap-time
    routing.

b7b418b1  ₢BBAAM    DRY the joint-length check in zrbrr_enforce. Prior commit had ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} repeated 4 times, ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} repeated 4 times, and the (prefix + 2 + moniker) sum computed inline twice (once in the test, once in the buc_die message). Factored to three local -r bindings: z_prefix_len, z_moniker_len, z_joint_len. Test and message now reference each computed value once. The 'd-' literal still appears once in the arithmetic and once in the message body — different roles (calculation vs operator-facing explanation), kept both. Build green; rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast 97/97.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
4a66a4ce  ₢BBAAM    Step 2 follow-on cleanup of rbgp_depot_levy surfaced by post-edit review. (1) Auth/region consolidation: the 'Validate input parameters' wrapping step (renamed to 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' in the prior notch) authenticated once at line 578 to call the AR locations API for region validation, then the next step 'Authenticate as Payor' authenticated again at line 597 with a duplicate 'local z_token' declaration and identical zrbgp_authenticate_capture call — the first token was unused after the region check. Reordered: 'Authenticate as Payor' becomes step 1 with the single 'local z_token' capture, 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' becomes step 2 reusing that token. One auth, one token, dependency between the two steps now explicit (region-check requires authenticated AR access). (2) Dead RBRP env-var checks dropped: the three 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die ...' / RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID / RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID lines under the old 'Authenticate as Payor' step were belt-and-suspenders dead code — zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish already validates these are non-empty before any rbgp command runs (rbgp_cli.sh line 63), and Step 2 unconditionalizing zrbrr_enforce makes the analogous-pattern asymmetry stand out. Removed all three. (3) Joint-length validation added at the right layer: zrbrr_enforce (rbrr_regime.sh) gains a custom check after the CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX format checks asserting $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} )) -le 30, with a buc_die message naming each component's length and the joint sum so an operator can immediately see which knob to dial back. The previous inline 30-char guard in levy fired only after auth + region API + project body construction; the regime-kindle layer catches the failure before any GCP API call. Worst-case math under existing individual caps: CLOUD_PREFIX max 11 (incl. trailing hyphen) + 'd-'(2) + MONIKER max 26 = 39 chars; the joint cap excludes the 30..39 sliver from regime validity. P2 #4 (RBAGS rename) withdrawn after rechecking AXLA-Lexicon.adoc:51 — RBAGS is an active umbrella xref ('Recipe Bottle Admin Google Specification'), used consistently across three buc_doc_brief sites (payor_install, depot_levy, payor_refresh); not stale, leaving as-is. Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97). Existing fast-suite negative tests don't probe the new joint-length check (the rbrr-bad-cloud-prefix-too-long case fires on individual-length cap before joint sum matters); a future case 'rbrr-joint-length-too-long' would round out coverage but adds a Rust fixture surface, deferred.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
ee816373  ₢BBAAM    Step 2 of rbrr-depot-identity-collapse: rbgp_depot_levy reshape to zero-arg, RBDC-driven. Levy drops the CLI positional arg (z_depot_name capture, regex/length validation block, RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX-anchored param doc) and reads RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER from the kindled regime. Drops the timestamp generation block (date scratch-file dance, ${PREFIX}-d-${NAME}-${TIMESTAMP} project-id construction, 30-char length guard, buf_write_fact RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID). All inline derivations replaced with RBDC kindle constants: ${z_depot_project_id} -> ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID} (12 sites), z_pool_stem local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM}, z_build_bucket local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GCS_BUCKET}, z_repository_name local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}; z_mason_name retained as ${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} per paddock's Mason-SA-name-unchanged lock. Display-name strings (project displayName, mason display name) switched from z_depot_name to RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER. Region validation block keeps its API call but the wrapping buc_step renames from 'Validate input parameters' to 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations'. Post-levy banner trimmed to single confirmation line: drops buc_step 'Display depot configuration' and the 5 buc_bare echo block (Project/Region/GAR repo/Pool stem/Mason SA), leaves buc_success and the Next: mantle-governor hint. rbgp_payor_install Next: hint reshaped from 'rbgp_depot_levy <depot-name> <region>' to 'rbgp_depot_levy' plus a follow-up directing the operator to set RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER and RBRR_GCP_REGION in rbrr.env first. rbgp_cli.sh differential-furnish skip removed: the case-block bypassing zrbrr_enforce for rbgp_depot_levy (annotated Step-2-pending in Step 1) and the Step-2-pending comment both dropped; zrbrr_enforce now runs unconditionally between zrbrr_kindle and zrbdc_kindle. Now-unused local z_command in zrbgp_furnish dropped. RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX dropped from rbgc_Constants.sh — sole consumer was levy's deleted regex/length block (in-flight decision per docket: dropped because no other consumer remained). RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID bash constant dropped from rbgc_Constants.sh and the matching mapping entry plus definition block dropped from RBS0-SpecTop.adoc; the Rust pristine fixture's RBTDRP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID consumer is now orphan-by-call (already service-tier-broken pending Step 5 reshape per Step 1 commit's STEP-5-PENDING annotation). Levy tabtarget tt/rbw-dL.PayorLeviesDepot.sh unchanged: its standard 'exec ... "${0##*/}" "${@}"' pattern matches every other zero-arg tabtarget (rbw-dl, rbw-dU); levy is now genuinely zero-arg, so the pattern is no longer asymmetric. No rbho_/rbhp_ handbook references to levy CLI args found. Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58 unit tests), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97 across enrollment-validation/regime-validation/regime-smoke/handbook-render), vow-b. Step 3 (rbgp_depot_unmake reshape) is the next pace; pristine fixture (rbtdrp_pristine.rs cases 2/3) widens its Step-5 fix surface by needing case-2 moniker-set, both cases positional-arg removal, and project_id derivation from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER replacing the dropped fact-file read. RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc operation spec remains stale (still describes CLI arg + timestamp + 30-char check) — out of Step 2 scope, separate pace material.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65	
66	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
67	
68	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
69	
70	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
71	
72	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
73	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
74	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
75	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
76	
77	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
78	
79	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
80	
81	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
82	
83	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
84	
85	## References
86	
87	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
88	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
89	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
90	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
91	
92	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAM
93	
94	## Character
95	
96	Cycle-driven debug. The five-step depot-identity-collapse implementation landed
97	(commits ee816373 → d2dec554). Remaining work is verifying it against live GCP
98	by stepping through what RELEASE.md *would* prescribe — except this pace does
99	not have the full RELEASE.md tabtarget set yet (canonical entry point `rbw-tP`
100	is unbuilt; step-3 inner sequence beyond the pristine-lifecycle fixture is
101	unreachable). Cycles run against the buildable subset.
102	
103	Load-bearing discipline: each turn does exactly one cycle — execute the next
104	reachable step, surface what fails, diagnose root cause, recommend fix shape,
105	then stop. No auto-fix. The stop keeps the operator in the loop and prevents
106	the drift where mechanical fixes paper over architectural questions.
107	
108	## Scope
109	
110	- Step through reachable RELEASE.md-equivalent today: Payor health probe →
111	  marshal-zero + commit → pristine-lifecycle fixture (cases 1, 2, 3).
112	- Out-of-reach this pace: canonical infrastructure setup, supply chain
113	  (inscribe/ordain), Crucible suite, cleanup. Those become reachable once
114	  `rbw-tP` is drafted in a follow-on pace.
115	- Per surfaced issue, route disposition: notch into BBAAM if in-scope-blocking
116	  the verification path, slate as separate pace if structural or out-of-scope,
117	  capture as itch if speculative.
118	
119	## Constraints
120	
121	- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract (waived to main for
122	  initial debug per operator).
123	- No fix lands without explicit operator nod after diagnosis is described.
124	- Diagnosed-but-unfixed issues accumulate as scar candidates for wrap-time
125	  routing.
126

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAM

## Character

Cycle-driven debug. The five-step depot-identity-collapse implementation landed
(commits ee816373 → d2dec554). Remaining work is verifying it against live GCP
by stepping through what RELEASE.md *would* prescribe — except this pace does
not have the full RELEASE.md tabtarget set yet (canonical entry point `rbw-tP`
is unbuilt; step-3 inner sequence beyond the pristine-lifecycle fixture is
unreachable). Cycles run against the buildable subset.

Load-bearing discipline: each turn does exactly one cycle — execute the next
reachable step, surface what fails, diagnose root cause, recommend fix shape,
then stop. No auto-fix. The stop keeps the operator in the loop and prevents
the drift where mechanical fixes paper over architectural questions.

## Scope

- Step through reachable RELEASE.md-equivalent today: Payor health probe →
  marshal-zero + commit → pristine-lifecycle fixture (cases 1, 2, 3).
- Out-of-reach this pace: canonical infrastructure setup, supply chain
  (inscribe/ordain), Crucible suite, cleanup. Those become reachable once
  `rbw-tP` is drafted in a follow-on pace.
- Per surfaced issue, route disposition: notch into BBAAM if in-scope-blocking
  the verification path, slate as separate pace if structural or out-of-scope,
  capture as itch if speculative.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract (waived to main for
  initial debug per operator).
- No fix lands without explicit operator nod after diagnosis is described.
- Diagnosed-but-unfixed issues accumulate as scar candidates for wrap-time
  routing.

file.content:
buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s to cancel (Ctrl-C)"
  sleep 1
  printf '%b ' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
  local z_i
  for (( z_i=z_seconds; z_i>=1; z_i-- )); do
    printf '%d... ' "$z_i" >/dev/tty
    sleep 1
  done
  printf '\n' >/dev/tty
}

buc_require() {
  local z_prompt="$1"
  local z_required_value="$2"

  if test "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" = "skip"; then
    buc_step "Confirm: (skipped — BURE_CONFIRM=skip)"
    return 0
  fi

  test -z "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" || buc_die "BURE_CONFIRM must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_CONFIRM}'"

  sleep 1
  printf '%b\n' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
  printf 'Type %s to confirm: ' "${z_required_value}" >/dev/tty
  local z_input
  read -r z_input </dev/tty
  test "${z_input}" = "${z_required_value}" || buc_die "Confirmation failed — expected '${z_required_value}', got '${z_input}'"
}

buc_execute() {
  set -e
  local prefix="$1"
  local title="$2"
  local env_func="$3"
  local command="${4:-}"
  shift 3; test -z "${command}" || shift

  export BUC_VERBOSE="${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}"

content.text:
Stopping per cycle-driven discipline — clear diagnosis, awaiting your nod.

**Outcome of this cycle:**

✅ Last cycle's four-CLI fix verified — marshal-zero progressed past kindling, executed all destructive steps cleanly, and **auto-committed** the result: `[main 972e1dc3] Marshal Zero — release qualification reset` (19 files, 31/31).

❌ New surface — `rblm_cli.sh:226` dies with `RBZ_ONBOARDING: unbound variable` after the auto-commit succeeds. Marshal-zero exits non-zero, breaking the fail-fast caller contract `rbw-tP` will rely on.

**Diagnosis (root cause):**

`rblm_cli.sh:226`:
```
buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARDING}"
```

That constant is referenced exactly once (only this site) and **never defined**. The actual constant is `RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE`, registered in `rbz_zipper.sh:85` and pointing at `rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh`. Pure typo — wrote the family's intuitive name instead of the registered name.

**Fix shape:**

One-line rename at `rblm_cli.sh:226`:
- `RBZ_ONBOARDING` → `RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE`

**Routing per docket:**

In-scope-blocking the verification path (marshal-zero exit code is the fail-fast signal `rbw-tP` will consume). Same shape as last cycle's four-CLI sweep — surfaced during verification, routes into ₢BBAAM rather than reopening anything upstream.

**Side note (TTY discovery, scar candidate)**: `buc_require` reads `/dev/tty` directly with no probe for non-tty stdin — the `BURE_CONFIRM=skip` bypass exists but only the implementer would know. Probably wants a tty-detection auto-skip or a clearer error pointing at the env var. Not in-scope for this pace; flagging for wrap-time scar routing.

**Authorization to proceed:**

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Payor - Billing and Destructive Operations CLI

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zrbgp_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgi_IAM.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrp_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgp_Payor.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Initialize modules'
  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  zrbgc_kindle

  source "${RBBC_rbrp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRP: ${RBBC_rbrp_file}"
  zrbrp_kindle
  zrbrp_enforce

  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbgi_kindle
  zrbgp_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbgp_ "Recipe Bottle Payor" zrbgp_furnish "$@"

# eof

content.text:
**Stopping per cycle-driven discipline. Case 2 surfaced an architectural Step-2-eagerness regression.**

**Outcome:**

❌ Case 2 (`rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle`) failed at the very first invocation — `depot_list 'list-pre'` probe — exit 1. Trace at `/var/folders/.../rbtd-84456/`. Stderr captures: `[rbgp_depot_list] RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER: RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty`.

**Diagnosis (root cause):**

Step 2 of BBAAM eliminated the differential-furnish skip in `rbgp_cli.sh` (commit ee816373):

> "rbgp_cli.sh differential-furnish skip removed: ... zrbrr_enforce now runs unconditionally between zrbrr_kindle and zrbdc_kindle."

That decision was correct for `rbgp_depot_levy` and `rbgp_depot_unmake` (both consume `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` directly via `${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}`). But it's wrong for `rbgp_depot_list` — Step 4 (commit 8eb44097) reshaped depot_list as a **cross-moniker** operation that scans every project matching `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-*` and emits per-moniker fact files. depot_list does not consume `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` and cannot, semantically — it walks all depots, not a chosen one.

The pristine fixture's autodetect design (Step 5, commit d2dec554) is **chicken-and-egg-locked** by this:
- `pick_next_moniker` needs fact files
- fact files come from `depot_list`
- `depot_list` (currently) needs a moniker
- there's no moniker until `pick_next_moniker` runs

So Step 5's case 2 flow `install_throwaway_prefixes → depot_list 'list-pre' → pick_next_moniker → install_depot_moniker → ...` is unreachable as-is. Step 2 made `zrbrr_enforce` strict; Step 4 added a cross-moniker consumer that needs it loose. The two steps didn't account for each other.

**Fix shape — two viable options:**

**Option A (tactical, smallest delta — matches just-removed pattern):** Reintroduce a case-block in `rbgp_cli.sh:zrbgp_furnish` that branches on the dispatched command. For `rbgp_depot_list`: skip `zrbdc_kindle` (no moniker-derived constants needed) and substitute a moniker-relaxed enforce variant. For all other commands: full enforce as today.

**Option C (architectural, named contracts):** Split `zrbrr_enforce` into `zrbrr_enforce_minimal` (prefix + runtime_prefix + region — the universal contract) and `zrbrr_enforce_moniker` (adds moniker format + joint-length checks — for depot-specific commands). Same split for `zrbdc_kindle` (no kindle if moniker absent). Each rbgp command declares which contract it needs. This generalizes — likely other commands (e.g., `rbgp_payor_install`) also don't need moniker but currently do.

A is tighter for this pace; C is the load-bearing answer if the differential-furnish pattern is going to recur.

**Routing:**

In-scope-blocking the verification path. Same shape as last two cycles — surfaced during verification, routes into ₢BBAAM. (Genuinely structural, but the docket precedent is in-scope-blocking wins for cycle-driven debug.)

**Two side notes** (scar candidates, not in-scope):

1. The Step 4 commit message describes depot_list as "single-shot per dispatch by design — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist." If depot_list runs successfully in `list-pre`, then again in `list-present`, it'll fatal at the second invocation since the same monikers reappear. The fixture has 3 depot_list calls; 2-onward will fail even after we fix the moniker issue. May need separate dispatches OR fact-file cleanup between calls.

2. Case 3 (`rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle`) almost certainly hits the same surface — same `list-pre` probe at start of flow.

**Awaiting nod:** which option (A or C), and authorization to proceed.

content.text:
Yes, your read is essentially right with one small caveat. Let me confirm what depot_list actually touches:

**RBRR fields depot_list uses:**
- `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` — used at `rbgp_Payor.sh:163` (`z_prefix_match="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-"` for the projects.list filter). **This one is load-bearing** — can't be empty or the filter becomes `d-*` and matches every project.

**RBRR fields depot_list does NOT use:**
- `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` — only relevant for one-depot ops (levy/unmake)
- `RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX` — bottle-side
- joint-length (prefix + moniker) — moot without moniker

**RBDC fields depot_list uses:** none (RBDC composes from moniker, depot_list walks the API instead)

**RBRP** — yes, needed for Payor OAuth. Already handled by `zrbrp_enforce` at lines 61-62, runs **after** the RBRR/RBDC block, so that's independent.

So skipping `zrbrr_enforce` AND `zrbdc_kindle` for depot_list works — the caller becomes responsible for `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` being set correctly. In fixture context, `install_throwaway_prefixes` sets it before the probe. In operator context, post-onboarding rbrr.env has it. Both paths covered.

**Refined fix shape (your simplification, fully spelled out):**

In `rbgp_cli.sh:zrbgp_furnish`, dispatch on command name (`$1` against the rbgp_-prefixed function family). For `rbgp_depot_list`:
- run `zrbrr_kindle` (loads the values)
- **skip** `zrbrr_enforce`
- **skip** `zrbdc_kindle`
- proceed with `zrbgc_kindle`, RBRP source/kindle/enforce, etc.

For all other commands: today's full sequence.

This matches the just-removed Step 1 differential-furnish pattern in shape (case-block on command), but with `depot_list` getting the relaxed path instead of `depot_levy` getting the relaxed path.

**Caveat / minor risk:** depot_list now silently tolerates a malformed `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` (e.g., uppercase, hyphens, empty). At worst you get zero matches or weird API behavior — no destruction, no cross-tenant bleed inside one Payor account. Acceptable trade for unblocking the autodetect chicken-and-egg.

**Authorization to apply this fix?** I'll add the case-block, run case 2 to confirm, then case 3.

message.content: Uh oh... lets look to see if there are ways to amend depot list to not need the cloud prefix to list.  Doesn't RBRP give it a 'master project' context that lets it iterate all related depots?

content.text:
Honest answer: **partly yes, but it's a structural shift, not a small tweak.** Let me walk through what RBRP does and doesn't give us.

**RBRP's actual scope:**
- `RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID` — the Payor's own project (a peer of depot projects, not their parent)
- `RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` — the OAuth client identity
- `RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID` — billing account every depot is linked to

The Payor project is **not** a resource-hierarchy parent of depots. There's no folder/org tying them together. The only cross-cutting linkages between Payor and depots are:
1. **Shared billing account** — every depot is `setBillingInfo`'d against `RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID` at levy time
2. **Shared owner identity** — Payor's OAuth-authenticated user created them

**So three architecturally cleaner paths exist, all structural:**

**Path 1: Billing-account scope.** Cloud Billing API has `billingAccounts/{id}/projects.list` returning all projects billed to that account. We'd query that, then classify each by Mason SA presence (existing classifier). Pros: zero CLOUD_PREFIX dependency. Cons: returns the Payor project too (need to filter), returns any other unrelated projects sharing the billing account (rare but possible — operator's other side projects), and lifecycle-state semantics differ (DELETE_REQUESTED projects may stop being billed).

**Path 2: Project labels.** `depot_levy` adds a label at creation: `rb-depot=true` (or richer: `rb-cloud-prefix=<prefix>` for multi-operator disambiguation). depot_list calls `projects.search?query=labels.rb-depot=true`. Pros: clean, label-based filtering, can encode multiple discriminators. Cons: requires retrofit — depots created pre-label can't be listed this way, and the label itself becomes load-bearing (an operator dropping it from rbrr.env regen breaks the listing).

**Path 3: Owner-identity scope.** projects.search supports `createTime`, but not really `creator` cleanly. Less promising than 1 or 2.

**The semantic shift:**

Today's depot_list semantic: *"depots under MY CLOUD_PREFIX namespace."* This means an operator with two CLOUD_PREFIX values in two repos (e.g., personal + work) sees them as separate depot universes — which is intentional isolation.

Path 1 semantic: *"all depots billed to my Payor account."* Cross-prefix visibility — both universes show up in one list. This is **different** from today, and may or may not be what we want for the multi-operator / multi-prefix story.

Path 2 with label `rb-depot=true` only: same semantic as Path 1.
Path 2 with label `rb-cloud-prefix=<prefix>`: same semantic as today, but storage moved from name → label.

**My honest read:**

The user-facing semantic — "list MY depots, scoped to MY chosen prefix namespace" — is reasonable. The naming-convention filter is a fine way to implement that semantic. The bug isn't that depot_list NEEDS CLOUD_PREFIX; that's correct. The bug is that depot_list got swept into demanding `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (a single-depot identifier) when it's a cross-moniker operation.

**Routing recommendation:**

- **In-scope-blocking fix (this cycle, ₢BBAAM)**: differential furnish for depot_list — skip moniker validation + `zrbdc_kindle`, keep CLOUD_PREFIX. Unblocks pristine fixture cases 2/3.
- **Slate as separate pace (architectural)**: "depot_list independence — billing-account scope or label-based filtering." This is a real design conversation worth having on its own — touches depot_levy (label emission), depot_list (filter source), retrofit (existing depots), and the multi-prefix story.

Want me to apply the tactical fix and slate the architectural pace as a separate sibling under ₣BB?

message.content: What if we add an RBRP prefix that gets used in place of CLOUD_PREFIX for its depots?  But hold on, why do we even need a prefix to list all depots?  Can't we use a full wildcard?  Why is this so hard?

content.text:
You're right — we **are** overthinking this. Your instinct is correct.

**The honest truth:**

Listing genuinely doesn't need a prefix. The **Mason SA presence is the load-bearing depot signature** — that's what distinguishes a "depot project" from any other project. The prefix-based filter is convenience, not correctness.

The Mason SA name pattern (`mason-${moniker}@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com`) is RB-specific enough that if the SA is present, it IS a depot. No prefix needed.

**Why is the current code structured this way?**

Pre-filter for cheaper API calls. The prefix filter narrows the project set BEFORE the per-project Mason SA probe, so we don't probe SAs across projects that obviously aren't depots. For an operator with a handful of projects, this optimization is meaningless. For one with 1000s, it matters. We've been treating an optimization as a contract.

**The cleanest fix shape (single localized change):**

Reshape `rbgp_depot_list` to scope by **billing account**, not name prefix:

1. Replace `projects.list?filter=id:${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-*` (today, lines ~163-180-ish in `zrbgp_depot_state_emit`) with `billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects.list` (Cloud Billing v1 API).
2. For each returned project, run the existing Mason SA classifier.
3. Project lacks Mason SA → not a depot, skip silently.
4. Project has Mason SA → emit fact file as today (COMPLETE/BROKEN/DELETE_REQUESTED).
5. Moniker for fact-file naming is derived from the Mason SA name (`mason-${moniker}` → strip prefix), not from the project name — so even if naming convention drifts, the fact files still key off the load-bearing identity.

**What this gets us:**
- depot_list needs **zero RBRR fields** — fully decouples from local-station regime
- depot_list needs RBRP only (for OAuth + billing account ID)
- The pristine fixture's chicken-and-egg dissolves: no moniker, no prefix, just "what's billed to my Payor account that has a Mason SA"
- The semantic also gets BETTER: an operator with two CLOUD_PREFIXes in two checkouts sees ALL their depots, not just the current checkout's slice. Probably what they actually want.

**Rust fixture impact:**
- `pick_next_moniker` still works — walks fact files emitted by depot_list, picks next-free per family stem. No change.
- `install_throwaway_prefixes` still happens (levy NEEDS prefix to mint a unique project ID), but it can run AFTER depot_list 'list-pre' rather than before. Sequence becomes: depot_list 'list-pre' (no prefix needed) → pick_next_moniker → install_throwaway_prefixes + install_depot_moniker → levy → ... 

**Routing question:**

This is more architectural than the differential-furnish band-aid, but it's *also* more localized — one function reshape vs. cli-dispatch case-block. And it dissolves the bug class rather than papering over it.

I'd argue this still routes into ₢BBAAM as in-scope-blocking — the cycle's whole point is to find Step-4 incompleteness like this. The paddock's locked design decisions don't conflict (CLOUD_PREFIX scope is for resource CREATION; this only changes LISTING semantics).

**Authorization check:**

Two questions for you:
1. Sound right? Or do you see a downside I'm missing?
2. If sound — apply this directly into ₢BBAAM, or do you want to slate it as a sibling pace (e.g., ₢BBAAN) so the change has its own clean docket / commit boundary separate from the typo-class fixes?

input.content:
# jjezs_slate depot-list-decouple-from-rbrr

## Character

Design conversation requiring judgment. Current `rbgp_depot_list` filters by `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-*` — treating the prefix-based naming convention as a contract when it is actually a convenience. The load-bearing depot signature is **Mason SA presence** (`mason-${moniker}@*.iam.gserviceaccount.com`), not the project name shape.

Surfaced during ₢BBAAM verification cycle: the pristine-lifecycle fixture's autodetect chain (`depot_list 'list-pre' → pick_next_moniker → install_depot_moniker → levy`) is chicken-and-egg-locked because Step 2's `zrbrr_enforce` unconditionalization made depot_list demand `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`, but depot_list is a cross-moniker operation that semantically cannot have one.

Diagnosis options surveyed during ₢BBAAM cycle (preserved here so this pace inherits the reasoning):
- *Differential furnish band-aid* (skip enforce + RBDC for depot_list, keep CLOUD_PREFIX filter): unblocks fixture, papers over the architectural mismatch, leaves CLOUD_PREFIX as a load-bearing input to a cross-moniker query.
- *Move prefix to RBRP* (RBRP-scoped namespace): collides with paddock's locked CLOUD_PREFIX-scope decision.
- *Drop the prefix filter entirely, scope by billing account, classify by Mason SA* (this pace): cleanest, dissolves the bug class.

Operator decision: take the architectural path. ₢BBAAM verification waits for this pace to land first, then resumes against the reshaped depot_list.

## Scope

Implementation:

- Reshape `rbgp_depot_list` and helper `zrbgp_depot_state_emit` in `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh`: scope query via `billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects` (Cloud Billing v1 API) instead of CRM v1 `projects.list` with `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-*` filter.
- Replace classifier discriminator: project is a depot iff a Mason SA (`mason-*@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com`) exists. Lifecycle classification (`RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE` / `_BROKEN` / `_DELETE_REQUESTED`) keeps existing logic.
- Derive fact-file moniker from Mason SA name (`mason-${moniker}` strip prefix), not from project name. Robust against naming-convention drift; aligns the fact-file key with the load-bearing depot identity.
- Remove `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` dependency from depot_list code path entirely.
- `Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh:zrbgp_furnish` adjustment: differential dispatch for `rbgp_depot_list` — skip `zrbrr_enforce` and `zrbdc_kindle` (no RBRR fields needed). All other commands keep today's full sequence. Pattern shape mirrors the Step-1 differential-furnish that Step 2 removed, but inverted: depot_list gets the relaxed path; levy/unmake keep the strict path.

Spec update:

- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` depot_list section: document the billing-account scope, the Mason SA classifier as the load-bearing discriminator, the loss of CLOUD_PREFIX dependency, and the listing-semantic shift. Reconcile any prose that conflates "name prefix" with "depot identity." Cross-check related sections (depot_levy, depot_unmake, RegimeRepo, the locked-design-decision callouts) for follow-on prose drift.
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDL-depot_list.adoc` operation spec sync: known stale per ₢BBAAM Step 4's commit notes; this is the natural place to discharge that staleness.
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc` — verify Mason SA contract still reads correctly given the SA is now a depot's primary identity from the listing perspective.

Fixture flow update:

- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbtdrp_pristine.rs` cases 2/3: optionally reorder to put `depot_list 'list-pre'` BEFORE `install_throwaway_prefixes` (no longer dependency-locked). Existing order also works since RBRR is now irrelevant to depot_list — pick whichever reads cleaner.
- Verify `rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker` still works against the new fact-file shape (file naming and content shape unchanged; only derivation source changes). Family-stem floor logic (`pristq` / `pristg` + 6-digit numeric) holds.
- Case 1 `rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation` unaffected (no depot_list call).

Listing-semantic shift to document explicitly:

- *Today*: "depots under MY `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` namespace."
- *New*: "depots billed to MY Payor account."
- Operators with multiple `CLOUD_PREFIX` values across different checkouts (e.g., personal + work) will see ALL their depots in one list, instead of just the current checkout's slice. This is almost certainly what they want; flag the change in the runbook regardless.

## Constraints

- Paddock's locked depot-identity-collapse decisions stand. `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` still scopes depot **CREATION** (project_id namespace, GAR repo name, pool stem, bucket name). This pace only changes **LISTING** semantics. The two are decoupled.
- Cycle-driven discipline carries forward: each turn does one cycle (one design step, one implementation step, or one spec section), surfaces what fails or what trade-off it creates, and stops for operator nod. Mass-edit-without-review is not the shape here even though the change is structural.
- No fix lands without explicit operator nod after diagnosis described.
- This pace blocks ₢BBAAM. ₢BBAAM verification path is paused until this pace closes, at which point ₢BBAAM re-runs pristine cases 2/3 against the reshaped depot_list.
- Mason SA classifier robustness: assumes `mason-` prefix is RB-exclusive. Verify no upstream GCP service mints SAs with that prefix; if it does, classifier needs a tighter discriminator (e.g., specific suffix or label). Surface as itch if not blocking.

## Out of scope

- Multi-operator visibility rules beyond the implicit OAuth-identity scope. If a billing account is shared across operators, all their depots will surface in each operator's list — this is a separate-pace conversation if it ever matters.
- Project labels (e.g., `rb-depot=true`) as an additional filter axis. Considered during diagnosis; deferred — Mason SA presence is sufficient.
- Payor naming uniformity (paddock-deferred at heat scope).

## References

- ₢BBAAM Step 4 (commit 8eb44097) — current depot_list implementation that this pace reshapes
- ₢BBAAM Step 5 (commit d2dec554) — pristine-lifecycle fixture autodetect that surfaced the chicken-and-egg
- ₢BBAAM verification cycle commits (425e00de, bbe29817) — typo-class fixes from cycle-driven debug; this pace's surface is the architectural sibling
- Cloud Billing API v1: `billingAccounts.projects.list`
- Paddock §"Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions" — CLOUD_PREFIX scope-for-creation lock; not violated by this pace

- `Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh:zrbgp_furnish` adjustment: differential dispatch for `rbgp_depot_list` — skip `zrbrr_enforce` and `zrbdc_kindle` (no RBRR fields needed). All other commands keep today's full sequence. Pattern shape mirrors the Step-1 differential-furnish that Step 2 removed, but inverted: depot_list gets the relaxed path; levy/unmake keep the strict path.

Spec update:

- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` depot_list section: document the billing-account scope, the Mason SA classifier as the load-bearing discriminator, the loss of CLOUD_PREFIX dependency, and the listing-semantic shift. Reconcile any prose that conflates "name prefix" with "depot identity." Cross-check related sections (depot_levy, depot_unmake, RegimeRepo, the locked-design-decision callouts) for follow-on prose drift.
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDL-depot_list.adoc` operation spec sync: known stale per ₢BBAAM Step 4's commit notes; this is the natural place to discharge that staleness.
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc` — verify Mason SA contract still reads correctly given the SA is now a depot's primary identity from the listing perspective.

Fixture flow update:

- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbtdrp_pristine.rs` cases 2/3: optionally reorder to put `depot_list 'list-pre'` BEFORE `install_throwaway_prefixes` (no longer dependency-locked). Existing order also works since RBRR is now irrelevant to depot_list — pick whichever reads cleaner.
- Verify `rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker` still works against the new fact-file shape (file naming and content shape unchanged; only derivation source changes). Family-stem floor logic (`pristq` / `pristg` + 6-digit numeric) holds.
- Case 1 `rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation` unaffected (no depot_list call).

Listing-semantic shift to document explicitly:

- *Today*: "depots under MY `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` namespace."
- *New*: "depots billed to MY Payor account."
- Operators with multiple `CLOUD_PREFIX` values across different checkouts (e.g., personal + work) will see ALL their depots in one list, instead of just the current checkout's slice. This is almost certainly what they want; flag the change in the runbook regardless.

## Constraints

- Paddock's locked depot-identity-collapse decisions stand. `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` still scopes depot **CREATION** (project_id namespace, GAR repo name, pool stem, bucket name). This pace only changes **LISTING** semantics. The two are decoupled.
- Cycle-driven discipline carries forward: each turn does one cycle (one design step, one implementation step, or one spec section), surfaces what fails or what trade-off it creates, and stops for operator nod. Mass-edit-without-review is not the shape here even though the change is structural.
- No fix lands without explicit operator nod after diagnosis described.
- This pace blocks ₢BBAAM. ₢BBAAM verification path is paused until this pace closes, at which point ₢BBAAM re-runs pristine cases 2/3 against the reshaped depot_list.
- Mason SA classifier robustness: assumes `mason-` prefix is RB-exclusive. Verify no upstream GCP service mints SAs with that prefix; if it does, classifier needs a tighter discriminator (e.g., specific suffix or label). Surface as itch if not blocking.

## Out of scope

- Multi-operator visibility rules beyond the implicit OAuth-identity scope. If a billing account is shared across operators, all their depots will surface in each operator's list — this is a separate-pace conversation if it ever matters.
- Project labels (e.g., `rb-depot=true`) as an additional filter axis. Considered during diagnosis; deferred — Mason SA presence is sufficient.
- Payor naming uniformity (paddock-deferred at heat scope).

## References

- ₢BBAAM Step 4 (commit 8eb44097) — current depot_list implementation that this pace reshapes
- ₢BBAAM Step 5 (commit d2dec554) — pristine-lifecycle fixture autodetect that surfaced the chicken-and-egg
- ₢BBAAM verification cycle commits (425e00de, bbe29817) — typo-class fixes from cycle-driven debug; this pace's surface is the architectural sibling
- Cloud Billing API v1: `billingAccounts.projects.list`
- Paddock §"Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions" — CLOUD_PREFIX scope-for-creation lock; not violated by this pace

Heat ordering is now:
- **₢BBAAS** (first, new) — depot-list-decouple-from-rbrr
- ... (existing paces including ₢BBAAM mid-cycle, paused until BBAAS closes)

The docket carries the full diagnosis context from this session — three options surveyed, the architectural choice locked, scope spelled out across implementation (rbgp_Payor.sh + rbgp_cli.sh), spec (RBS0 + RBSDL + cross-check on RBSCIG), and Rust fixture (rbtdrp_pristine.rs). Cycle-driven discipline preserved.

**Working tree status:** clean (jjx_enroll auto-committed the gallops state).

**Next move — your call:**
- Mount ₢BBAAS now and start the design conversation (probably a model-tier shift to opus-architectural for the Cloud Billing API + spec discussion)?
- Pick it up in a follow-on session?
- Anything else before we step away from ₢BBAAM mid-pause?

9038b3d7 jjb:1011-4d810db7:₢BBAAQ:n: Relocate two collaboration-posture bullets from project CLAUDE.md Collaboration Style section into jjk-claude-context.md for portability — if Job Jockey is installed in another project, the posture guidance travels with the kit rather than being lost on the source side. (1) jjk-claude-context.md: inserted two bold-prefixed inline notes after the Concepts block and before "Identities vs Display Names", matching the existing peer-note pattern (Concepts/Identities/Case sensitivity/MCP Tool Usage are all bold-prefixed inline). "**Sincerity over efficiency:**" + body about saying-what-you-notice and discovery-through-conversation. The first bullet's original "This collaborator values sincerity over efficiency" preamble dropped because the bold heading is the gloss — redundant. Substance preserved verbatim otherwise. (2) CLAUDE.md: removed the entire "### Collaboration Style" section header and both bullets. Section ceased to exist (was already only those two bullets). Working Preferences section now flows directly into Heredoc Delimiter Selection without the intervening Collaboration Style header. Reasoning emerged across this BBAAQ session: I made up a verbose continuation prompt with pre-decided voicings and pre-listed constants; user diagnosed the failure as me imposing my outline instead of pointing at the docket; we walked back through CLAUDE.md to nudge against that failure mode (commit 32676ca5 demoted Character from a section to a line in CLAUDE.md). Then this commit relocates that already-edited bullet, plus the sincerity bullet, into the JJK include because the user's framing shifted from "slim CLAUDE.md" to "all Job Jockey ideas need to live in the JJK include for portability" (includes auto-load, so accessibility is unchanged; cohesiveness improves). Tools/jjk/CLAUDE.md (kit-internal hygiene note) cautions against modifying the parent project's CLAUDE.md from a kit-development workflow; not blocking here because this is a project-level placement decision by the project owner crossing the boundary deliberately, not a kit-development edit. Five unrelated dirty files in working tree at notch time (rbgc_Constants.sh, rbgp_Payor.sh, rbgp_cli.sh, RBS0-SpecTop.adoc, RBSDL-depot_list.adoc) appear to be another officium's BBAAS depot-list-decouple-from-rbrr work — not mine, not in this commit per CLAUDE.md additive-only discipline.
4d810db7 jjb:1011-66cfc8f9:₢BBAAS:n: Reshape rbgp_depot_list so the depot signature is Mason SA presence, not project-name prefix. zrbgp_depot_state_emit now queries Cloud Billing API v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects, lists service accounts per project via IAM v1, classifies a project as a depot iff a service account matching ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com$ exists, derives moniker by stripping the mason- prefix from the SA's local-part, looks up lifecycle via CRM v1 projects/${id}, and emits per-moniker fact files. RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is no longer emitted — Mason SA presence is the discriminator, so a project missing a Mason SA is not classified as a depot at all. State machine collapses to {COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED}; the BROKEN constant remains in rbgc_Constants.sh for backward compatibility but no code path emits it. New constant RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT='depot-project' added next to RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT — emit now writes a sidecar fact file '<moniker>.depot-project' carrying the canonical project_id since project_id can no longer be composed from ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${moniker}; rbgp_depot_list render reads both fact files keyed by moniker. RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX is fully removed from the depot_list code path. zrbgp_furnish in rbgp_cli.sh gets differential dispatch via local -r z_command='${1:-}' — when the command is rbgp_depot_list, zrbrr_enforce and zrbdc_kindle are skipped (zrbrr_kindle still runs to source rbrr.env into env, which is harmless when the file is absent or fields are blank); levy/unmake/install/governor-mantle keep the strict path. Spec sweep: RBSDL-depot_list.adoc fully rewritten to describe the new Billing v1 → IAM SA list → Mason classifier → CRM lifecycle → dual-fact-file flow, BROKEN state removed, COMPLETE/DELETE_REQUESTED only in the display summary; RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbtoe_depot_list_update pattern updated to the same new flow (Billing v1 enumeration, Mason SA classifier, CRM lifecycle lookup, dual fact-file emission), dropping the old prose about CRM filter 'projectId:rbw-depot-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE' and the rbw-[name]-[timestamp] pattern validation. RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc audited — no listing-semantics prose to update; left untouched. Mason SA discriminator robustness: research (general-purpose agent + WebSearch) confirmed mason- prefix is not used by any GCP-managed service or common IaC pattern; residual false-positive risk only from a customer accidentally naming an unrelated SA mason-X under the same billing account, mitigated by anchoring the moniker portion of the discriminator regex to RBRR's ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape. Constants used everywhere instead of hardcoded literals (caught during BCG review: agent's first pass had 'mason-' literal at three sites; fixed to ${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}- with proper inner-quoting in the # operator per BCG:410-415). BCG review also accepted: if-test (vs case) for differential furnish on a single excluded command; || true on CRM lookup with capture-or-default fallback (less explicit than the SA list path's code-check, but works); regex moniker portion left unbounded (RBRR caps at 26 — defensible tightening for a future itch, not blocking). Pre-existing inherited patterns (SC2231/SC2295 on the render glob and parameter expansion) confirmed unchanged from old code via git show HEAD. CLAUDE.md modified by another officium left untouched per additive-only discipline. Listing-semantic shift to flag in operator-facing runbooks: today 'depots under MY CLOUD_PREFIX namespace' → new 'depots billed to MY Payor account'. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite regime-validation fixture: 24/27 pass; 3 failures are rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) all failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' — pre-existing post-marshal-zero environmental state from commit 972e1dc3, none of these tests exercise rbgp_* code, so they cannot be regressions from this pace. Verification of pristine-lifecycle cases 2/3 against the reshaped depot_list (the chicken-and-egg surface that prompted this pace) is service-tier and deferred to ₢BBAAM verification cycle resumption — that pace was paused waiting for this one to land.

28250cb1 jjb:1011-84f9f7d8:₢BBAAQ:n: Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in §"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: — concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch — second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: — implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation — out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: — instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: → :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file → busff_multi_fact → buf_write_fact_single → buf_write_fact_multi → buf_burx_env → buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) — not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ pending: Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb concepts from BBAAN paddock-settled vocabulary), Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention), and the BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.
