28250cb1 jjb:1011-84f9f7d8:₢BBAAQ:n: Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in §"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: — concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch — second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: — implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation — out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: — instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: → :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file → busff_multi_fact → buf_write_fact_single → buf_write_fact_multi → buf_burx_env → buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) — not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ pending: Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb concepts from BBAAN paddock-settled vocabulary), Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention), and the BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.

BCG review also accepted: if-test (vs case) for differential furnish on a single excluded command; || true on CRM lookup with capture-or-default fallback (less explicit than the SA list path's code-check, but works); regex moniker portion left unbounded (RBRR caps at 26 — defensible tightening for a future itch, not blocking). Pre-existing inherited patterns (SC2231/SC2295 on the render glob and parameter expansion) confirmed unchanged from old code via git show HEAD. CLAUDE.md modified by another officium left untouched per additive-only discipline. Listing-semantic shift to flag in operator-facing runbooks: today 'depots under MY CLOUD_PREFIX namespace' → new 'depots billed to MY Payor account'. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite regime-validation fixture: 24/27 pass; 3 failures are rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) all failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' — pre-existing post-marshal-zero environmental state from commit 972e1dc3, none of these tests exercise rbgp_* code, so they cannot be regressions from this pace. Verification of pristine-lifecycle cases 2/3 against the reshaped depot_list (the chicken-and-egg surface that prompted this pace) is service-tier and deferred to ₢BBAAM verification cycle resumption — that pace was paused waiting for this one to land.

 Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh                |   1 +
 Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh                    | 144 +++++++++++++++++++----------
 Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh                      |   8 +-
 Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc     |  29 +++---
 Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDL-depot_list.adoc |  56 ++++++++---
 5 files changed, 160 insertions(+), 78 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh b/Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh
index daf560ab..dc4ac223 100755
--- a/Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh
+++ b/Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh
@@ -141,15 +141,19 @@ zrbgp_authenticate_capture() {
 }
 
 # Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
-# Each ACTIVE depot's Mason SA presence distinguishes COMPLETE from BROKEN;
-# DELETE_REQUESTED projects are recorded as such. Per-moniker fact files
-# named "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" are written via buf_write_fact_multi
-# with state content drawn from RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_*; consumers walk the
-# emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
+# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
+# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
+# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
+# DELETE_REQUESTED projects are recorded as such.  Per-moniker fact files
+# named "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" (state) and
+# "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" (project_id) are written via
+# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
+# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
 #
-# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist, so a
-# second call within the same dispatch would fatal at the first repeated
-# moniker. By design: one tabtarget = one dispatch = one emit call.
+# RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is never emitted — Mason SA presence IS the
+# discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are silently skipped.
+#
+# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
 #
 # Args: <oauth_access_token>
 zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
@@ -158,59 +162,105 @@ zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
   local -r z_token="${1:-}"
   test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"
 
-  # No lifecycleState filter — DELETE_REQUESTED projects must surface so
-  # their per-moniker fact files emit alongside ACTIVE ones.
-  local -r z_prefix_match="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-"
-  local -r z_filter="projectId:${z_prefix_match}*"
-  local -r z_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects?filter=${z_filter// /%20}"
-  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_state_list"
-  rbgu_http_require_ok "List depot projects" "depot_state_list"
+  # RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is min=0 in the regime (optional for payor-only
+  # operations) but required here to enumerate depot projects.
+  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
+    || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"
+
+  # Query Cloud Billing API v1: list all projects under the billing account.
+  local -r z_billing_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects"
+  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_billing_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_state_billing"
+  rbgu_http_require_ok "List billing account projects" "depot_state_billing"
 
   local z_project_count
-  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" '.projects // [] | length') || z_project_count=0
+  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
+    || z_project_count=0
 
   test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0 || return 0
 
   # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2 — declare outside, assign inside)
   local z_index=0
   local z_project_id=""
+  local z_billing_enabled=""
+  local z_sa_list_infix=""
+  local z_sa_list_code=""
+  local z_sa_count=""
+  local z_sa_index=0
+  local z_sa_email=""
+  local z_mason_email=""
+  local z_mason_local=""
   local z_moniker=""
+  local z_crm_infix=""
   local z_lifecycle_state=""
   local z_state=""
-  local z_mason_email=""
-  local z_mason_url=""
-  local z_mason_infix=""
-  local z_mason_code=""
 
   while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
-    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" ".projects[${z_index}].projectId") \
+    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
       || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }
 
-    if [[ "${z_project_id}" != "${z_prefix_match}"* ]]; then
-      buc_log_args "Skipping project outside this installation's depot namespace: ${z_project_id}"
+    z_billing_enabled=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].billingEnabled") \
+      || z_billing_enabled="false"
+
+    if test "${z_billing_enabled}" != "true"; then
+      buc_log_args "Skipping project with billing disabled: ${z_project_id}"
+      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
+      continue
+    fi
+
+    # List service accounts in this project; treat HTTP errors as skip (not fatal).
+    z_sa_list_infix="depot_state_sa_${z_index}"
+    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
+      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts" \
+      "${z_token}" "${z_sa_list_infix}" || true
+
+    z_sa_list_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}") || z_sa_list_code=""
+    if test "${z_sa_list_code}" != "200"; then
+      buc_log_args "Skipping project (SA list HTTP ${z_sa_list_code}): ${z_project_id}"
       z_index=$((z_index + 1))
       continue
     fi
-    z_moniker="${z_project_id#"${z_prefix_match}"}"
 
-    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_list" ".projects[${z_index}].lifecycleState // \"UNKNOWN\"") \
+    z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
+      || z_sa_count=0
+
+    # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion enforced to RBRR's
+    # ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape so an unrelated SA literally named e.g.
+    # "mason-Foo" cannot be misclassified as a depot.
+    z_mason_email=""
+    z_sa_index=0
+    while test "${z_sa_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
+      z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" ".accounts[${z_sa_index}].email") \
+        || { z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1)); continue; }
+      if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@[^@]*\.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$ ]]; then
+        z_mason_email="${z_sa_email}"
+        break
+      fi
+      z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1))
+    done
+
+    if test -z "${z_mason_email}"; then
+      buc_log_args "Skipping project — no Mason SA found: ${z_project_id}"
+      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
+      continue
+    fi
+
+    # Derive moniker from Mason SA local-part (strip leading mason- prefix)
+    z_mason_local="${z_mason_email%%@*}"
+    z_moniker="${z_mason_local#"${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-"}"
+
+    # Determine lifecycle state via CRM v1.
+    z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_index}"
+    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
+      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
+      "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true
+
+    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_crm_infix}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') \
       || z_lifecycle_state="UNKNOWN"
 
     if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
       z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
     elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
-      z_mason_email=$(rbgu_sa_email_capture "${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${z_moniker}" "${z_project_id}") \
-        || buc_die "Failed to compose Mason email for moniker ${z_moniker}"
-      z_mason_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
-      z_mason_infix="depot_state_mason_${z_index}"
-      rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_mason_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_mason_infix}" || true
-
-      z_mason_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_mason_infix}") || z_mason_code=""
-      if test "${z_mason_code}" = "200"; then
-        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
-      else
-        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN}"
-      fi
+      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
     else
       buc_log_args "Skipping moniker ${z_moniker} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
       z_index=$((z_index + 1))
@@ -218,6 +268,7 @@ zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
     fi
 
     buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" "${z_state}"
+    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" "${z_project_id}"
 
     z_index=$((z_index + 1))
   done
@@ -1019,10 +1070,8 @@ rbgp_depot_list() {
   zrbgp_depot_state_emit "${z_token}"
 
   buc_step 'Render depot summary'
-  local -r z_prefix_match="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-"
   local z_total_count=0
   local z_complete_count=0
-  local z_broken_count=0
   local z_delete_requested_count=0
 
   buc_info ""
@@ -1034,22 +1083,26 @@ rbgp_depot_list() {
   local z_basename=""
   local z_moniker=""
   local z_project_id=""
+  local z_project_fact_path=""
   local z_state=""
 
-  # Walk emitted fact files; filename stem is the moniker, content is the state.
+  # Walk emitted depot fact files; stem is the moniker, content is the state.
+  # The sidecar depot-project fact file carries the canonical project_id.
   shopt -s nullglob
   for z_fact_path in "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"/*.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}; do
     z_basename="${z_fact_path##*/}"
     z_moniker="${z_basename%.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}}"
-    z_project_id="${z_prefix_match}${z_moniker}"
+    z_project_fact_path="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_moniker}.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}"
     z_state=$(<"${z_fact_path}")
     test -n "${z_state}" || buc_die "Empty state in fact file: ${z_fact_path}"
+    test -f "${z_project_fact_path}" || buc_die "Missing depot-project fact file: ${z_project_fact_path}"
+    z_project_id=$(<"${z_project_fact_path}")
+    test -n "${z_project_id}" || buc_die "Empty project_id in fact file: ${z_project_fact_path}"
 
     printf "%-40s %s\n" "${z_project_id}" "${z_state}"
     z_total_count=$((z_total_count + 1))
     case "${z_state}" in
       "${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}")          z_complete_count=$((z_complete_count + 1)) ;;
-      "${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN}")            z_broken_count=$((z_broken_count + 1)) ;;
       "${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}")  z_delete_requested_count=$((z_delete_requested_count + 1)) ;;
     esac
   done
@@ -1064,14 +1117,7 @@ rbgp_depot_list() {
   buc_info "=== SUMMARY ==="
   buc_info "Total depots:     ${z_total_count}"
   buc_info "Complete:         ${z_complete_count}"
-  buc_info "Broken:           ${z_broken_count}"
   buc_info "Delete-requested: ${z_delete_requested_count}"
-
-  if test "${z_broken_count}" -gt 0; then
-    buc_info ""
-    buc_info "Note: BROKEN depots may have missing resources (Mason SA, repository, or bucket)"
-    buc_info "Consider investigating broken depots or destroying them if no longer needed"
-  fi
 }
 
 rbgp_payor_oauth_refresh() {

content.content:
commit 4d810db719f2389323d56ae7fd7c3f1253ccd9d3
Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
Date:   Mon Apr 27 11:34:46 2026 -0700

    jjb:1011-66cfc8f9:₢BBAAS:n: Reshape rbgp_depot_list so the depot signature is Mason SA presence, not project-name prefix. zrbgp_depot_state_emit now queries Cloud Billing API v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects, lists service accounts per project via IAM v1, classifies a project as a depot iff a service account matching ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com$ exists, derives moniker by stripping the mason- prefix from the SA's local-part, looks up lifecycle via CRM v1 projects/${id}, and emits per-moniker fact files. RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is no longer emitted — Mason SA presence is the discriminator, so a project missing a Mason SA is not classified as a depot at all. State machine collapses to {COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED}; the BROKEN constant remains in rbgc_Constants.sh for backward compatibility but no code path emits it. New constant RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT='depot-project' added next to RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT — emit now writes a sidecar fact file '<moniker>.depot-project' carrying the canonical project_id since project_id can no longer be composed from ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${moniker}; rbgp_depot_list render reads both fact files keyed by moniker. RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX is fully removed from the depot_list code path. zrbgp_furnish in rbgp_cli.sh gets differential dispatch via local -r z_command='${1:-}' — when the command is rbgp_depot_list, zrbrr_enforce and zrbdc_kindle are skipped (zrbrr_kindle still runs to source rbrr.env into env, which is harmless when the file is absent or fields are blank); levy/unmake/install/governor-mantle keep the strict path. Spec sweep: RBSDL-depot_list.adoc fully rewritten to describe the new Billing v1 → IAM SA list → Mason classifier → CRM lifecycle → dual-fact-file flow, BROKEN state removed, COMPLETE/DELETE_REQUESTED only in the display summary; RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbtoe_depot_list_update pattern updated to the same new flow (Billing v1 enumeration, Mason SA classifier, CRM lifecycle lookup, dual fact-file emission), dropping the old prose about CRM filter 'projectId:rbw-depot-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE' and the rbw-[name]-[timestamp] pattern validation. RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc audited — no listing-semantics prose to update; left untouched. Mason SA discriminator robustness: research (general-purpose agent + WebSearch) confirmed mason- prefix is not used by any GCP-managed service or common IaC pattern; residual false-positive risk only from a customer accidentally naming an unrelated SA mason-X under the same billing account, mitigated by anchoring the moniker portion of the discriminator regex to RBRR's ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape. Constants used everywhere instead of hardcoded literals (caught during BCG review: agent's first pass had 'mason-' literal at three sites; fixed to ${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}- with proper inner-quoting in the # operator per BCG:410-415). BCG review also accepted: if-test (vs case) for differential furnish on a single excluded command; || true on CRM lookup with capture-or-default fallback (less explicit than the SA list path's code-check, but works); regex moniker portion left unbounded (RBRR caps at 26 — defensible tightening for a future itch, not blocking). Pre-existing inherited patterns (SC2231/SC2295 on the render glob and parameter expansion) confirmed unchanged from old code via git show HEAD. CLAUDE.md modified by another officium left untouched per additive-only discipline. Listing-semantic shift to flag in operator-facing runbooks: today 'depots under MY CLOUD_PREFIX namespace' → new 'depots billed to MY Payor account'. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite regime-validation fixture: 24/27 pass; 3 failures are rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) all failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' — pre-existing post-marshal-zero environmental state from commit 972e1dc3, none of these tests exercise rbgp_* code, so they cannot be regressions from this pace. Verification of pristine-lifecycle cases 2/3 against the reshaped depot_list (the chicken-and-egg surface that prompted this pace) is service-tier and deferred to ₢BBAAM verification cycle resumption — that pace was paused waiting for this one to land.

diff --git a/Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh b/Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh
index 2b4be616..34d784dc 100755
--- a/Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh
+++ b/Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh
@@ -154,6 +154,7 @@ zrbgc_kindle() {
   # rbgp_depot_list emits one fact file per known depot named "<moniker>.depot"
   # with content equal to one of the state values below.
   readonly RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT="depot"
+  readonly RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT="depot-project"
   readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE="COMPLETE"
   readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN="BROKEN"
   readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED="DELETE_REQUESTED"
diff --git a/Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh b/Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh
index e4596b4e..97cf642a 100755
--- a/Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh
+++ b/Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh
@@ -27,6 +27,8 @@ zrbgp_furnish() {
   buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
   buc_doc_env_done || return 0
 
+  local -r z_command="${1:-}"
+
   source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
   local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
   source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
@@ -52,8 +54,10 @@ zrbgp_furnish() {
   zrbcc_kindle
 
   zrbrr_kindle
-  zrbrr_enforce
-  zrbdc_kindle
+  if test "${z_command}" != "rbgp_depot_list"; then
+    zrbrr_enforce
+    zrbdc_kindle
+  fi
 
   zrbgc_kindle

Mason SA discriminator robustness: research (general-purpose agent + WebSearch) confirmed mason- prefix is not used by any GCP-managed service or common IaC pattern; residual false-positive risk only from a customer accidentally naming an unrelated SA mason-X under the same billing account, mitigated by anchoring the moniker portion of the discriminator regex to RBRR's ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape. Constants used everywhere instead of hardcoded literals (caught during BCG review: agent's first pass had 'mason-' literal at three sites; fixed to ${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}- with proper inner-quoting in the # operator per BCG:410-415). BCG review also accepted: if-test (vs case) for differential furnish on a single excluded command; || true on CRM lookup with capture-or-default fallback (less explicit than the SA list path's code-check, but works); regex moniker portion left unbounded (RBRR caps at 26 — defensible tightening for a future itch, not blocking). Pre-existing inherited patterns (SC2231/SC2295 on the render glob and parameter expansion) confirmed unchanged from old code via git show HEAD. CLAUDE.md modified by another officium left untouched per additive-only discipline. Listing-semantic shift to flag in operator-facing runbooks: today 'depots under MY CLOUD_PREFIX namespace' → new 'depots billed to MY Payor account'. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite regime-validation fixture: 24/27 pass; 3 failures are rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) all failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' — pre-existing post-marshal-zero environmental state from commit 972e1dc3, none of these tests exercise rbgp_* code, so they cannot be regressions from this pace. Verification of pristine-lifecycle cases 2/3 against the reshaped depot_list (the chicken-and-egg surface that prompted this pace) is service-tier and deferred to ₢BBAAM verification cycle resumption — that pace was paused waiting for this one to land.

 Discovered constraints from implementation:
 +

+    {rbtgo_director_invest} grants `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` at project scope.
     Vouch runs on the same private pool as conjure (the depot's worker pool, derived from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`), eliminating an untested default-pool-with-buildx permutation.
   * **gcloud exception**: Vouch verification (conjure mode) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe` for provenance retrieval (see {rbsk_no_gcloud} exception).
     All other steps use the GCE metadata server for OAuth tokens and direct registry REST API calls.

 exists or
 {rbbc_fatal}
 "Director RBRA file missing - run
-{rbtgo_director_knight}",

 . loads RBRA credentials (RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL, RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY, RBRA_PROJECT_ID),
 . generates JWT with scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`,
 . {rbbc_call} link:https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token[Google OAuth token endpoint] to exchange JWT for access token,
@@ -3754,7 +3756,7 @@ service account context:
 exists or
 {rbbc_fatal}
 "Retriever RBRA file missing - run
-{rbtgo_retriever_charter}",
+{rbtgo_retriever_invest}",
 . loads RBRA credentials (RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL, RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY, RBRA_PROJECT_ID),
 . generates JWT with scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`,
 . {rbbc_call} link:https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token[Google OAuth token endpoint] to exchange JWT for access token,
@@ -3788,16 +3790,17 @@ A pattern that loads and validates RBRO credentials:
 {rbtoe_depot_list_update}::
 A pattern that synchronizes
 {rbtgi_depot}
-project tracking:
+fact-file state after a depot lifecycle mutation:
 
 . executes
 {rbtoe_payor_authenticate}
 to obtain access token,
-. {rbbc_call} link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/list[projects.list] with filter `projectId:rbw-depot-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE`,
-. {rbbc_require} HTTP 200 response from projects API,
-. extracts project IDs from `.projects[].projectId` array,
-. validates each project ID matches pattern `rbw-[name]-[timestamp]`,
-. constructs space-delimited string from valid depot project IDs,
+. {rbbc_call} Cloud Billing v1 `billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects` to enumerate projects with `billingEnabled=true`,
+. for each billed project, {rbbc_call} IAM v1 `projects.serviceAccounts.list` and inspects SA emails for a Mason SA matching `mason-«MONIKER»@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com`,
+. skips projects with no matching Mason SA; derives moniker by stripping the `mason-` prefix,
+. {rbbc_call} CRM v1 `projects/${project_id}` to look up lifecycle state for each Mason-SA-bearing project,
+. classifies state: `ACTIVE` → `COMPLETE`, `DELETE_REQUESTED` → `DELETE_REQUESTED`; other states are logged and skipped,
+. emits per-moniker fact files: `«MONIKER».depot` (state) and `«MONIKER».depot-project` (canonical project_id),
 . invoked by
 {rbtgo_payor_install},
 {rbtgo_depot_levy},

RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX is fully removed from the depot_list code path. zrbgp_furnish in rbgp_cli.sh gets differential dispatch via local -r z_command='${1:-}' — when the command is rbgp_depot_list, zrbrr_enforce and zrbdc_kindle are skipped (zrbrr_kindle still runs to source rbrr.env into env, which is harmless when the file is absent or fields are blank); levy/unmake/install/governor-mantle keep the strict path. Spec sweep: RBSDL-depot_list.adoc fully rewritten to describe the new Billing v1 → IAM SA list → Mason classifier → CRM lifecycle → dual-fact-file flow, BROKEN state removed, COMPLETE/DELETE_REQUESTED only in the display summary; RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbtoe_depot_list_update pattern updated to the same new flow (Billing v1 enumeration, Mason SA classifier, CRM lifecycle lookup, dual fact-file emission), dropping the old prose about CRM filter 'projectId:rbw-depot-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE' and the rbw-[name]-[timestamp] pattern validation. RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc audited — no listing-semantics prose to update; left untouched. Mason SA discriminator robustness: research (general-purpose agent + WebSearch) confirmed mason- prefix is not used by any GCP-managed service or common IaC pattern; residual false-positive risk only from a customer accidentally naming an unrelated SA mason-X under the same billing account, mitigated by anchoring the moniker portion of the discriminator regex to RBRR's ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape. Constants used everywhere instead of hardcoded literals (caught during BCG review: agent's first pass had 'mason-' literal at three sites; fixed to ${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}- with proper inner-quoting in the # operator per BCG:410-415). BCG review also accepted: if-test (vs case) for differential furnish on a single excluded command; || true on CRM lookup with capture-or-default fallback (less explicit than the SA list path's code-check, but works); regex moniker portion left unbounded (RBRR caps at 26 — defensible tightening for a future itch, not blocking). Pre-existing inherited patterns (SC2231/SC2295 on the render glob and parameter expansion) confirmed unchanged from old code via git show HEAD. CLAUDE.md modified by another officium left untouched per additive-only discipline. Listing-semantic shift to flag in operator-facing runbooks: today 'depots under MY CLOUD_PREFIX namespace' → new 'depots billed to MY Payor account'. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite regime-validation fixture: 24/27 pass; 3 failures are rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) all failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' — pre-existing post-marshal-zero environmental state from commit 972e1dc3, none of these tests exercise rbgp_* code, so they cannot be regressions from this pace. Verification of pristine-lifecycle cases 2/3 against the reshaped depot_list (the chicken-and-egg surface that prompted this pace) is service-tier and deferred to ₢BBAAM verification cycle resumption — that pace was paused waiting for this one to land.

content.text:
Reviewed commit 4d810db7 — the BBAAS work landed in parallel with the slate (another officium took it on, completed implementation + spec). Build green, fast-suite-deferred, pristine cases 2/3 deferred to BBAAM resumption.

**Concerns:**

1. **Pagination missing.** Both `billingAccounts.projects.list` and `projects.serviceAccounts.list` return `nextPageToken`; code reads first page only. Operators with billing accounts holding >50 projects, or any project with many SAs, will see truncated listings — depots silently invisible.

2. **DELETE_REQUESTED visibility likely broken.** Code filters projects with `billingEnabled=true`; GCP typically disables billing as part of lifecycle teardown, so DELETE_REQUESTED projects probably never reach the Mason-SA classifier. The dual-state design is meant to surface graveyard depots so moniker-collision is visible — if billing-filtered out, that intent is defeated. Needs empirical verification against a real DELETE_REQUESTED depot.

3. **Out-of-scope RBS0 churn.** Mapping section gained `rbtgo_director_invest/divest/muster` and `rbtgo_retriever_invest/divest/muster`; lost `rbtgo_director_knight`, `rbtgo_retriever_charter`, `rbtgo_sa_forfeit`, `rbtgo_sa_list`. Two body references followed (lines 1828, 3738, 3756). Functions like `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_muster_directors` don't exist — appears to be another officium's in-flight rename accidentally swept in. Either complete it or back it out; right now the spec has dangling quoins.

4. **CRM lifecycle lookup unchecked.** `|| true` on the call, then `jq '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"'` — a transient 403/404/timeout looks identical to a real "UNKNOWN" lifecycle and silently drops the depot. Should mirror the SA-list path's explicit `rbgu_http_code_capture` + skip-with-log.

5. **Orphan `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN` constant.** Retained "for backward compatibility" but no producer or consumer remains. Dead code; violates load-bearing-complexity.

6. **API cost expansion.** Old: 1 CRM list + N IAM probes (2 RTTs per depot, narrowly scoped by prefix). New: 1 Billing list + N IAM-list-per-billed-project + M CRM lookups. For operators sharing a billing account with many non-RB projects, the per-project SA enumeration multiplies. Flag for QPS-conscious users, not blocking.

7. **Pristine fixture sidecar redundancy.** `rbtdrp_compose_project_id` (Rust, from BBAAM Step 5) re-derives `${cloud_prefix}d-${moniker}` for diagnostic tee. New emit writes canonical `${moniker}.depot-project` fact file. When BBAAM resumes, it should consume the sidecar and drop the derivation — keeps the fixture aligned with the new "Mason SA is the identity, not the name shape" stance.

8. **Listing-semantic shift not propagated to operator-facing prose.** Spec captures the shift; onboarding/handbook tracks don't mention it. Operators with multiple checkouts on one Payor account will see all depots, not just the current checkout's slice. May surprise.

9. **BBAAM resumption noise.** 3 fast-suite cases (`rv_rbrr_repo`, `rv_rbrv_all_vessels`, `rv_rbrn_all_nameplates`) currently fail on the marshal-zero environmental state. When BBAAM verification cycle resumes, those failures will obscure real regressions. BBAAM should re-establish a non-empty RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER (or the throwaway-prefix install path) before re-running fast suite, or accept those 3 as pre-known noise.

Items 1, 2, 3, 4 are the load-bearing ones — would block confident depot_list operation in any non-toy environment. The rest are hygiene / handoff concerns.

Help me decide on repairs appropriate for 5-9.

content.text:
**On DELETE_REQUESTED visibility:**

Dropping the `billingEnabled=true` filter is half the answer — necessary but possibly not sufficient. GCP behavior caveat: on DELETE_REQUESTED projects, IAM may refuse to list service accounts (project is read-only during the 30-day soft-delete window). If IAM fails, we silently skip → graveyard depots invisible regardless.

**Recommendation: drop the filter** and verify empirically. If IAM does list SAs on dormant projects, problem solved. If it doesn't, we need a different strategy (cache prior monikers, or accept the gap and document it). The latter is a separate-pace conversation; not BBAAS-blocking.

The CRM-first reordering also helps: query lifecycle BEFORE SA list, so on DELETE_REQUESTED we know we're looking at a dormant project and can either probe-anyway-and-skip-on-fail, OR accept the gap with clear logging. Cleaner than today's emit-or-skip-by-SA-only structure.

---

**Repairs 5–9:**

| # | Concern | Recommendation | Rationale |
|---|---------|---------------|-----------|
| 5 | Orphan `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN` constant | **Fix now (in BBAAS or tight follow-up)** | This pace orphaned it; load-bearing-complexity says delete. Trivial: rm constant + grep for any remaining doc references. |
| 6 | API cost expansion (1+N+M) | **Accept; capture as itch** | Performance, not correctness. No operator has hit it yet. If rate limits ever bite, revisit with `projects.search` filter or optional CLOUD_PREFIX pre-filter (re-coupling for performance is a defensible itch but not now). |
| 7 | Pristine fixture sidecar redundancy | **Defer to BBAAM resumption** | BBAAM owns the fixture surface. Replacing `rbtdrp_compose_project_id` with a sidecar read is small Rust work and aligns the fixture with the new "Mason SA is the identity" stance. Natural cycle for it. |
| 8 | Listing-semantic shift not in handbook prose | **Defer or itch** | Handbook reorg is a parallel effort. Bundle with next handbook-touch (cleaner than one-off). Low risk — operators with one checkout see no change. |
| 9 | BBAAM resumption noise (3 fast-suite env failures) | **Document in BBAAM's resumption notes, no code change** | Transient state from marshal-zero, not a defect. BBAAM's verification cycle should re-establish RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER (via throwaway-prefix install) before re-running fast suite, or explicitly note the 3 as pre-known noise. |

---

**Suggested action shape:**

A small follow-up notch on ₢BBAAS bundles:
- Drop `billingEnabled=true` filter (concern 2)
- Reorder: CRM lifecycle lookup BEFORE SA list (handles concerns 2 and 4 cleanly)
- Add `rbgu_http_code_capture` check on CRM call (concern 4)
- Add `nextPageToken` pagination on Billing list and IAM SA list (concern 1)
- Delete `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN` constant (concern 5)
- Capture concerns 6, 8 as itches under ₣BB

Concerns 7 and 9 routed into BBAAM's resumption docket as work-items, not BBAAS-side fixes.

Want me to enroll the follow-up notch's scope into BBAAS, or slate it as a sibling pace? BBAAS already has substantial commit weight — sibling pace gives a cleaner commit boundary for the correctness fixes.

message.content: add a sibling pace right after BBAAS now to repair these things.  Speak simply, don't bloat docket with aspects that are obvious when the work starts.

28250cb1 jjb:1011-84f9f7d8:₢BBAAQ:n: Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in §"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: — concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch — second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: — implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation — out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: — instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: → :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file → busff_multi_fact → buf_write_fact_single → buf_write_fact_multi → buf_burx_env → buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) — not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ pending: Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb concepts from BBAAN paddock-settled vocabulary), Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention), and the BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.

4d810db7 jjb:1011-66cfc8f9:₢BBAAS:n: Reshape rbgp_depot_list so the depot signature is Mason SA presence, not project-name prefix. zrbgp_depot_state_emit now queries Cloud Billing API v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects, lists service accounts per project via IAM v1, classifies a project as a depot iff a service account matching ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com$ exists, derives moniker by stripping the mason- prefix from the SA's local-part, looks up lifecycle via CRM v1 projects/${id}, and emits per-moniker fact files. 28250cb1 jjb:1011-84f9f7d8:₢BBAAQ:n: Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in §"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: — concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch — second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: — implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation — out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: — instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: → :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file → busff_multi_fact → buf_write_fact_single → buf_write_fact_multi → buf_burx_env → buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) — not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ pending: Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb concepts from BBAAN paddock-settled vocabulary), Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention), and the BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.

9af836de jjb:1011-f065916f:₢BBAAT:n: BBAAT depot-list-correctness-sweep against BBAAS landed code (commit 4d810db7). Five mechanical correctness fixes to zrbgp_depot_state_emit and one constant deletion. (1) Drop billingEnabled=true filter — DELETE_REQUESTED projects auto-disable billing, so the filter was hiding the very lifecycle the docket wanted observable. (2) Reorder CRM lifecycle lookup before IAM SA list — design conversation surfaced three options (a/b/c). Option (b) selected: CRM-first as a lifecycle gate, but still list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED so the empirical question 'does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?' stays observable rather than short-circuited away. CRM is the cheaper call and the conceptually-prior question ('is this project alive?'); SA list is the discriminator ('does it have a Mason SA?'). New shape: {Billing list → CRM lookup → lifecycle gate → SA list → Mason scan → emit} replacing {Billing list → billing-enabled filter → SA list → Mason scan → CRM lookup → emit}. (3) Add rbgu_http_code_capture check on CRM lookup — replaces 'rbgu_http_json ... || true' followed by jq '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"' fallback (silently lossy on HTTP errors). New path treats non-200 CRM as observability event with explicit log line, then continue. Lifecycle classification stays {ACTIVE → COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED → DELETE_REQUESTED, else → skip-with-log}. (4) Add nextPageToken pagination on Billing v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects and IAM v1 projects/${id}/serviceAccounts. Both wrap the call in a 'while :; do' loop with rbgu_urlencode_capture on the pageToken (BCG-blessed _capture function). Per-page infixes 'depot_state_billing_${page}' and 'depot_state_sa_${global_project_index}_${sa_page}' keep response files distinct. SA-list inner loop breaks on first Mason SA hit (depot's Mason SA is the singleton); on miss, advances page until nextPageToken empty. Global project index threads across billing-list pages so per-project CRM/SA infixes don't collide between pages. (5) Delete orphan RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN constant from rbgc_Constants.sh — Mason SA presence IS the discriminator post-BBAAS, BROKEN was never emitted, comment in rbgp_Payor.sh updated to drop the constant reference. BCG audit caught and fixed two violations in the agent's first draft: (a) 'test -n "${z_mason_email}" && break' inside the SA-list loop on Mason hit — BCG forbids '&& break/continue/return' for control flow (relies on set -e exemption); fixed to 'test -z "${z_mason_email}" || break' (inverted test, || form). (b) Raw '$(jq -r .nextPageToken // empty <file>)' for nextPageToken extraction — BCG forbids $() on external commands (the rbgg_Governor.sh precedent uses raw jq but predates rbgu_json_field_capture's existence); fixed to 'rbgu_json_field_capture <infix> .nextPageToken' which is a _capture function blessed for $(). Empirical check stays open per docket: behavior of IAM v1 serviceAccounts list against DELETE_REQUESTED projects is observable in the new code (DELETE_REQUESTED + Mason SA → moniker emitted; DELETE_REQUESTED + IAM-hides-SAs → 'no Mason SA' log); answers when service-tier verification (BBAAM) resumes. RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID requirement preserved (regime-min=0 but required at runtime here). Mason discriminator regex unchanged. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite 24/27 regime-validation matches BBAAS baseline exactly — same 3 rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' from blank-template post-marshal-zero state, none exercise rbgp_* code; not regressions from this pace. 47/47 enrollment-validation. BBAAM verification path unblocks: resumes at next service-tier credential window to re-run pristine cases 2/3 against the now-corrected depot_list, with the empirical IAM-on-DELETE_REQUESTED question observable in operator output.

c7babf0f jjb:1011-f500538f:₢AUAAZ:W: BCG compliance audit across 19 files in BUK/RBK: waves 1-2 (correctness/portability — pseudo-ternaries, [[ ]]→test, eval→indirect, unguarded commands, echo -e→printf), waves 3-4 (command substitution elimination — temp files, builtins, _capture functions). Post-audit cleanup: reverted RBGP function extraction that changed error contract, reverted 3 secret-to-disk conversions (private key, GitLab token, webhook secret), fixed digest whitespace stripping, fixed 5 test&&assignment patterns, replaced process substitution with temp file. 19/102 files covered — focused on highest-risk modules. Rule refinement deferred to ₢AUAAc.

commit 9af836de2ffbc9ca693b060d1a0defefb93840cd
Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
Date:   Mon Apr 27 12:06:42 2026 -0700

    jjb:1011-f065916f:₢BBAAT:n: BBAAT depot-list-correctness-sweep against BBAAS landed code (commit 4d810db7). Five mechanical correctness fixes to zrbgp_depot_state_emit and one constant deletion. (1) Drop billingEnabled=true filter — DELETE_REQUESTED projects auto-disable billing, so the filter was hiding the very lifecycle the docket wanted observable. (2) Reorder CRM lifecycle lookup before IAM SA list — design conversation surfaced three options (a/b/c). Option (b) selected: CRM-first as a lifecycle gate, but still list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED so the empirical question 'does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?' stays observable rather than short-circuited away. CRM is the cheaper call and the conceptually-prior question ('is this project alive?'); SA list is the discriminator ('does it have a Mason SA?'). New shape: {Billing list → CRM lookup → lifecycle gate → SA list → Mason scan → emit} replacing {Billing list → billing-enabled filter → SA list → Mason scan → CRM lookup → emit}. (3) Add rbgu_http_code_capture check on CRM lookup — replaces 'rbgu_http_json ... || true' followed by jq '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"' fallback (silently lossy on HTTP errors). New path treats non-200 CRM as observability event with explicit log line, then continue. Lifecycle classification stays {ACTIVE → COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED → DELETE_REQUESTED, else → skip-with-log}. (4) Add nextPageToken pagination on Billing v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects and IAM v1 projects/${id}/serviceAccounts. Both wrap the call in a 'while :; do' loop with rbgu_urlencode_capture on the pageToken (BCG-blessed _capture function). Per-page infixes 'depot_state_billing_${page}' and 'depot_state_sa_${global_project_index}_${sa_page}' keep response files distinct. SA-list inner loop breaks on first Mason SA hit (depot's Mason SA is the singleton); on miss, advances page until nextPageToken empty. Global project index threads across billing-list pages so per-project CRM/SA infixes don't collide between pages. (5) Delete orphan RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN constant from rbgc_Constants.sh — Mason SA presence IS the discriminator post-BBAAS, BROKEN was never emitted, comment in rbgp_Payor.sh updated to drop the constant reference. BCG audit caught and fixed two violations in the agent's first draft: (a) 'test -n "${z_mason_email}" && break' inside the SA-list loop on Mason hit — BCG forbids '&& break/continue/return' for control flow (relies on set -e exemption); fixed to 'test -z "${z_mason_email}" || break' (inverted test, || form). (b) Raw '$(jq -r .nextPageToken // empty <file>)' for nextPageToken extraction — BCG forbids $() on external commands (the rbgg_Governor.sh precedent uses raw jq but predates rbgu_json_field_capture's existence); fixed to 'rbgu_json_field_capture <infix> .nextPageToken' which is a _capture function blessed for $(). Empirical check stays open per docket: behavior of IAM v1 serviceAccounts list against DELETE_REQUESTED projects is observable in the new code (DELETE_REQUESTED + Mason SA → moniker emitted; DELETE_REQUESTED + IAM-hides-SAs → 'no Mason SA' log); answers when service-tier verification (BBAAM) resumes. RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID requirement preserved (regime-min=0 but required at runtime here). Mason discriminator regex unchanged. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite 24/27 regime-validation matches BBAAS baseline exactly — same 3 rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' from blank-template post-marshal-zero state, none exercise rbgp_* code; not regressions from this pace. 47/47 enrollment-validation. BBAAM verification path unblocks: resumes at next service-tier credential window to re-run pristine cases 2/3 against the now-corrected depot_list, with the empirical IAM-on-DELETE_REQUESTED question observable in operator output.

 Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh     | 220 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++-----------------
 2 files changed, 134 insertions(+), 87 deletions(-)

 # is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.

+# projects are candidates for SA-list scanning.  Per-moniker fact files

 # BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
 #
-# RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is never emitted — Mason SA presence IS the
-# discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are silently skipped.
+# Mason SA presence IS the discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are
+# silently skipped (no BROKEN state emitted).
+#
+# Billing v1 projects list and IAM v1 serviceAccounts list are paginated via
+# nextPageToken; per-page infixes keep response files distinct.
 #
 # Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
 #
@@ -167,110 +171,154 @@ zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
   test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
     || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"

-

-

+  local z_crm_code=""
+  local z_lifecycle_state=""
+  local z_state=""
+  local z_sa_url_base=""
+  local z_sa_url=""
+  local z_sa_page=1
+  local z_sa_page_token=""
+  local z_sa_tok_enc=""
+  local z_sa_infix=""
   local z_sa_list_code=""
-  local z_sa_count=""
+  local z_sa_count=0
   local z_sa_index=0
   local z_sa_email=""
   local z_mason_email=""
   local z_mason_local=""
   local z_moniker=""
-  local z_crm_infix=""
-  local z_lifecycle_state=""
-  local z_state=""
-
-  while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
-    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
-      || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }
-
-    z_billing_enabled=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].billingEnabled") \
-      || z_billing_enabled="false"
-
-    if test "${z_billing_enabled}" != "true"; then
-      buc_log_args "Skipping project with billing disabled: ${z_project_id}"
-      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
-      continue
-    fi
-
-    # List service accounts in this project; treat HTTP errors as skip (not fatal).
-    z_sa_list_infix="depot_state_sa_${z_index}"
-    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
-      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts" \
-      "${z_token}" "${z_sa_list_infix}" || true
 
-    z_sa_list_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}") || z_sa_list_code=""
-    if test "${z_sa_list_code}" != "200"; then
-      buc_log_args "Skipping project (SA list HTTP ${z_sa_list_code}): ${z_project_id}"
-      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
-      continue

+      z_billing_url="${z_billing_url}?pageToken=${z_billing_tok_enc}"
     fi
 
-    z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
-      || z_sa_count=0
-
-    # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion enforced to RBRR's
-    # ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape so an unrelated SA literally named e.g.
-    # "mason-Foo" cannot be misclassified as a depot.
-    z_mason_email=""
-    z_sa_index=0

+
+    z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
+      || z_project_count=0
+
+    z_index=0
+    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
+      z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
+        || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1)); continue; }
+
+      # CRM v1 lifecycle lookup gates the SA list — cheaper call, single
+      # classification decision before potentially-failing IAM scan.
+      z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_global_project_index}"
+      rbgu_http_json "GET" \
+        "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
+        "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true
+
+      z_crm_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_crm_infix}") || z_crm_code=""
+      if test "${z_crm_code}" != "200"; then
+        buc_log_args "Skipping project (CRM lookup HTTP ${z_crm_code}): ${z_project_id}"
+        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
+        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
+        continue
       fi
-      z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1))
-    done

-      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
-      continue
-    fi
+      z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_crm_infix}" '.lifecycleState') \
+        || z_lifecycle_state=""
+

+        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
+      elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
+        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
+      else
+        buc_log_args "Skipping project ${z_project_id} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"

+      # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion anchored to RBRR's

toolUseResult.stdout:
commit 9af836de2ffbc9ca693b060d1a0defefb93840cd
Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
Date:   Mon Apr 27 12:06:42 2026 -0700

    jjb:1011-f065916f:₢BBAAT:n: BBAAT depot-list-correctness-sweep against BBAAS landed code (commit 4d810db7). Five mechanical correctness fixes to zrbgp_depot_state_emit and one constant deletion. (1) Drop billingEnabled=true filter — DELETE_REQUESTED projects auto-disable billing, so the filter was hiding the very lifecycle the docket wanted observable. (2) Reorder CRM lifecycle lookup before IAM SA list — design conversation surfaced three options (a/b/c). Option (b) selected: CRM-first as a lifecycle gate, but still list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED so the empirical question 'does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?' stays observable rather than short-circuited away. CRM is the cheaper call and the conceptually-prior question ('is this project alive?'); SA list is the discriminator ('does it have a Mason SA?'). New shape: {Billing list → CRM lookup → lifecycle gate → SA list → Mason scan → emit} replacing {Billing list → billing-enabled filter → SA list → Mason scan → CRM lookup → emit}. (3) Add rbgu_http_code_capture check on CRM lookup — replaces 'rbgu_http_json ... || true' followed by jq '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"' fallback (silently lossy on HTTP errors). New path treats non-200 CRM as observability event with explicit log line, then continue. Lifecycle classification stays {ACTIVE → COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED → DELETE_REQUESTED, else → skip-with-log}. (4) Add nextPageToken pagination on Billing v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects and IAM v1 projects/${id}/serviceAccounts. Both wrap the call in a 'while :; do' loop with rbgu_urlencode_capture on the pageToken (BCG-blessed _capture function). Per-page infixes 'depot_state_billing_${page}' and 'depot_state_sa_${global_project_index}_${sa_page}' keep response files distinct. SA-list inner loop breaks on first Mason SA hit (depot's Mason SA is the singleton); on miss, advances page until nextPageToken empty. Global project index threads across billing-list pages so per-project CRM/SA infixes don't collide between pages. (5) Delete orphan RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN constant from rbgc_Constants.sh — Mason SA presence IS the discriminator post-BBAAS, BROKEN was never emitted, comment in rbgp_Payor.sh updated to drop the constant reference. BCG audit caught and fixed two violations in the agent's first draft: (a) 'test -n "${z_mason_email}" && break' inside the SA-list loop on Mason hit — BCG forbids '&& break/continue/return' for control flow (relies on set -e exemption); fixed to 'test -z "${z_mason_email}" || break' (inverted test, || form). (b) Raw '$(jq -r .nextPageToken // empty <file>)' for nextPageToken extraction — BCG forbids $() on external commands (the rbgg_Governor.sh precedent uses raw jq but predates rbgu_json_field_capture's existence); fixed to 'rbgu_json_field_capture <infix> .nextPageToken' which is a _capture function blessed for $(). Empirical check stays open per docket: behavior of IAM v1 serviceAccounts list against DELETE_REQUESTED projects is observable in the new code (DELETE_REQUESTED + Mason SA → moniker emitted; DELETE_REQUESTED + IAM-hides-SAs → 'no Mason SA' log); answers when service-tier verification (BBAAM) resumes. RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID requirement preserved (regime-min=0 but required at runtime here). Mason discriminator regex unchanged. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite 24/27 regime-validation matches BBAAS baseline exactly — same 3 rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' from blank-template post-marshal-zero state, none exercise rbgp_* code; not regressions from this pace. 47/47 enrollment-validation. BBAAM verification path unblocks: resumes at next service-tier credential window to re-run pristine cases 2/3 against the now-corrected depot_list, with the empirical IAM-on-DELETE_REQUESTED question observable in operator output.

content.content:
commit 9af836de2ffbc9ca693b060d1a0defefb93840cd
Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
Date:   Mon Apr 27 12:06:42 2026 -0700

    jjb:1011-f065916f:₢BBAAT:n: BBAAT depot-list-correctness-sweep against BBAAS landed code (commit 4d810db7). Five mechanical correctness fixes to zrbgp_depot_state_emit and one constant deletion. (1) Drop billingEnabled=true filter — DELETE_REQUESTED projects auto-disable billing, so the filter was hiding the very lifecycle the docket wanted observable. (2) Reorder CRM lifecycle lookup before IAM SA list — design conversation surfaced three options (a/b/c). Option (b) selected: CRM-first as a lifecycle gate, but still list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED so the empirical question 'does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?' stays observable rather than short-circuited away. CRM is the cheaper call and the conceptually-prior question ('is this project alive?'); SA list is the discriminator ('does it have a Mason SA?'). New shape: {Billing list → CRM lookup → lifecycle gate → SA list → Mason scan → emit} replacing {Billing list → billing-enabled filter → SA list → Mason scan → CRM lookup → emit}. (3) Add rbgu_http_code_capture check on CRM lookup — replaces 'rbgu_http_json ... || true' followed by jq '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"' fallback (silently lossy on HTTP errors). New path treats non-200 CRM as observability event with explicit log line, then continue. Lifecycle classification stays {ACTIVE → COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED → DELETE_REQUESTED, else → skip-with-log}. (4) Add nextPageToken pagination on Billing v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects and IAM v1 projects/${id}/serviceAccounts. Both wrap the call in a 'while :; do' loop with rbgu_urlencode_capture on the pageToken (BCG-blessed _capture function). Per-page infixes 'depot_state_billing_${page}' and 'depot_state_sa_${global_project_index}_${sa_page}' keep response files distinct. SA-list inner loop breaks on first Mason SA hit (depot's Mason SA is the singleton); on miss, advances page until nextPageToken empty. Global project index threads across billing-list pages so per-project CRM/SA infixes don't collide between pages. (5) Delete orphan RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN constant from rbgc_Constants.sh — Mason SA presence IS the discriminator post-BBAAS, BROKEN was never emitted, comment in rbgp_Payor.sh updated to drop the constant reference. BCG audit caught and fixed two violations in the agent's first draft: (a) 'test -n "${z_mason_email}" && break' inside the SA-list loop on Mason hit — BCG forbids '&& break/continue/return' for control flow (relies on set -e exemption); fixed to 'test -z "${z_mason_email}" || break' (inverted test, || form). (b) Raw '$(jq -r .nextPageToken // empty <file>)' for nextPageToken extraction — BCG forbids $() on external commands (the rbgg_Governor.sh precedent uses raw jq but predates rbgu_json_field_capture's existence); fixed to 'rbgu_json_field_capture <infix> .nextPageToken' which is a _capture function blessed for $(). Empirical check stays open per docket: behavior of IAM v1 serviceAccounts list against DELETE_REQUESTED projects is observable in the new code (DELETE_REQUESTED + Mason SA → moniker emitted; DELETE_REQUESTED + IAM-hides-SAs → 'no Mason SA' log); answers when service-tier verification (BBAAM) resumes. RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID requirement preserved (regime-min=0 but required at runtime here). Mason discriminator regex unchanged. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite 24/27 regime-validation matches BBAAS baseline exactly — same 3 rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' from blank-template post-marshal-zero state, none exercise rbgp_* code; not regressions from this pace. 47/47 enrollment-validation. BBAAM verification path unblocks: resumes at next service-tier credential window to re-run pristine cases 2/3 against the now-corrected depot_list, with the empirical IAM-on-DELETE_REQUESTED question observable in operator output.

test -n "${z_out}" || return 1

rbgu_json_field_capture() {
  zrbgu_sentinel
  local -r z_infix="${1}"
  local -r z_jq="${2}"
  local -r z_json_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local z_result
  z_result=$(jq -r "${z_jq}" "${z_json_file}")          || return 1
  if test -z "${z_result}" || test "${z_result}" = "null"; then return 1; fi
  echo "${z_result}"
}

rbgu_http_code_capture() {
  zrbgu_sentinel
  local -r z_infix="${1}"
  local -r z_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_CODE}"
  local z_code
  z_code=$(<"${z_file}") || return 1
  test -n "${z_code}"    || return 1
  echo "${z_code}"
}

content.text:
Reviewed commit `9af836de` (BBAAT notch).

**Verdict: solid. All five docket items addressed cleanly.**

**What's good:**

1. **CRM-first reordering** — option (b) from the design conversation, exactly: lifecycle gate before SA list, but DELETE_REQUESTED projects still flow into SA list so the empirical IAM-on-dormant question stays observable rather than short-circuited away. CRM is the cheaper / conceptually-prior call; SA list is the discriminator.

2. **CRM HTTP code check** — `rbgu_http_code_capture` mirrors the SA-list path. Non-200 logged + skipped explicitly. The earlier `// "UNKNOWN"` jq fallback is gone.

3. **Pagination** — both Billing v1 and IAM v1 SA list wrapped in `while :; do` with `rbgu_urlencode_capture` on page tokens. Per-page infixes (`depot_state_billing_${page}`, `depot_state_sa_${global_project_index}_${sa_page}`) keep response files distinct. Global project index correctly threads across billing pages so per-project CRM/SA infixes don't collide.

4. **`rbgu_json_field_capture`'s null handling verified** — reading rbgu_Utility.sh:197 confirms the function returns 1 on empty OR literal "null" result, so the `|| z_billing_page_token=""` fallback correctly terminates the page loop when the API omits `nextPageToken` on the last page. No infinite-loop risk.

5. **BCG audit landed two real fixes** — `&& break` → `test -z ... || break` (forbidden control-flow pattern), and raw `$(jq ...)` → `rbgu_json_field_capture` (blessed `_capture` form). Good self-discipline.

6. **Constant deletion clean** — one line removed from `rbgc_Constants.sh`, comment in `zrbgp_depot_state_emit` updated to no longer mention BROKEN. No dangling references.

7. **Inner SA-pagination break logic correct** — first Mason hit breaks both the inner SA loop and the outer page loop (singleton invariant of depot's Mason SA respected).

**Minor concerns (not blocking):**

1. **Double log on SA-list HTTP failure.** When IAM returns non-200, inner loop logs `"Skipping project (SA list HTTP X)"` and breaks. Then the outer `if test -z "${z_mason_email}"` check also fires, logging `"Skipping project — no Mason SA found"`. Operator sees two lines for the same project; the second is misleading (the SA list never returned, so we never had a chance to find a Mason SA). Trivial cleanup: a `z_sa_skip=1` flag set on HTTP-fail break, checked before the trailing log. Or just accept the noise — the truth is in the first line.

2. **Empirical IAM-on-DELETE_REQUESTED question unverified.** Correctly deferred to BBAAM service-tier resumption per docket. The new code makes the answer observable in the operator's stderr — that's the right shape.

3. **Temp-dir accumulation across pages.** Each page generates a JSON+code file pair under BURD_TEMP_DIR. For billing accounts with many pages, this is a few dozen files per dispatch. Not a leak (cleaned at dispatch end), just noting.

**Recommendation:** Wrap when ready. The double-log nit is fine to leave (or fold into a follow-up if/when the operator-experience pass happens) — not load-bearing for correctness, and BBAAM's service-tier run will exercise this code anyway, surfacing it naturally if it bothers the operator.
